name: Castro de Troña from the Castillo de Sobroso — circular by Monte Picaraña
name_original: Castro de Troña desde el Castillo de Sobroso — circular por el Monte Picaraña
region_intro: Hiking around Mondariz offers diverse landscapes characterized by lush riverine environments and dense forests, particularly around the Río Tea. The region features varied terrain, including granite formations and areas protected within the Natura 2000 network. These natural elements contribute to a setting suitable for outdoor exploration, with trails often following watercourses and traversing wooded areas. Best hiking trails around Mondariz The most popular hiking route is Río Barragan – Fraga trail (PR-G164), a 10.7 miles (17.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 49 minutes to complete. This moderate path explores river landscapes and dense forests. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Tea River Trail, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.6 km) path. This route allows exploration of verdant river environments and historical bridges. Local hikers also love the Castro de Troña from the Castillo de Sobroso — circular by Monte Picaraña, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain and historical sites, often completed in about 3 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Mondariz is defined by riverine environments, dense forests, and granite formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Mondariz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Mondariz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Mondariz?
A: Mondariz offers a diverse selection of over 45 hiking trails. This includes 16 easy routes perfect for a relaxed stroll, 29 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 2 difficult routes for experienced hikers looking for a demanding adventure.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes in Mondariz?
A: Hiking in Mondariz immerses you in lush riverine environments and dense forests, particularly along the Río Tea, which is part of the protected Natura 2000 network. You'll encounter varied terrain, including granite formations, and enjoy the serene beauty of the region's natural springs and waterways.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hikes in Mondariz?
A: Yes, Mondariz has several easy routes suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31896967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Amolares Footbridge – Parque das Tres Pontes loop from Mondariz Balneario&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 4 km long and features minimal elevation gain, making it perfect for a leisurely outing.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I discover while hiking?
A: Mondariz is rich in history. You can explore Roman-era bridges, such as the historic Puente de Cernadela, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10457500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de Gándara – Cernadela Bridge loop from Mondariz Balneario&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2499686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto San Nomedio Summit and Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, which may sit atop an older temple, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2886707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Coutada Petroglyphs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Mondariz are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375789463/castro-de-trona-from-the-castillo-de-sobroso-circular-by-monte-picarana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Troña from the Castillo de Sobroso — circular by Monte Picaraña&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and historical insights.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mondariz?
A: The autumn months are particularly beautiful for hiking in Mondariz, as the forests transform with vibrant colors and the sound of flowing water is distinct. However, the region's lush riverine environments and dense forests offer a pleasant experience throughout much of the year.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature the famous Río Tea?
A: Yes, the Río Tea is a central feature of the region's hiking. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375297838/rio-tea-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tea River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.6 km path that allows you to explore the verdant river environments and discover historical bridges along its banks.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mondariz?
A: The hiking routes in Mondariz are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the beauty of the riverine environments, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Can I find any viewpoints or natural monuments on the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several natural monuments and viewpoints. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2532948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de A Picaraña&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views, or explore the area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barragán River&lt;/a&gt;, which is known for its natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6288387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Galleiro&lt;/a&gt; also provides scenic vistas.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: While most trails are easy to moderate, there are 2 routes classified as difficult for those seeking a more strenuous experience. These trails typically involve significant elevation gain or longer distances, demanding very good fitness and sure-footedness.

Q: Are there any lakes or water features to see on hikes?
A: Beyond the rivers, you can find beautiful lakes and water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1449456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eiras Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6288413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Galleiro&lt;/a&gt; are notable spots that can be incorporated into longer hiking excursions or visited as nearby attractions.
tips:
Dating back to the late 11th century, teh castle has a long, rich history. In 1117, when Queen Urraca was surrounded in this castle by supporters of her son, the boy-king Alfonso VII, she managed to escape to Santiago de Compostela in search of the aid of Archbishop Gelmírez. Legend has it that she fled through the underground passageway that connected the castle to the banks of the Tea.

The castle tour can be done in an an hour or so. Check hours, they do siesta here...
The Monte de la Picaraña (in Galician Monte de A Picaraña) is a mountain located between the municipalities of Puenteareas and Mondariz.

The site dates from just over six hundred years before Christ, (the beginning of the use of stone as a building material). Like many of the regional settlements in Galicia, the Castro de Troña had its splendor around the 1st and 2nd centuries BC. This community most likely moved closer to the Tea river due to easier food and water sources. The arrival of the Romans also contributed to the abandonment of the site in search of better protected grounds.

The remains of numerous houses, some excavated, and others still hidden by earth and vegetation are scatted over the hilltop. Most of them have a circular plan and in others an additional larger social room of the higher social classes. We will also find square and rectangular floor plans at this site typical of Romanized settlements. The indigenous inhabitants did not make corners in their homes, due to the belief that evil spirits hid there. 

On a small rock, very close to the cistern, there is a famous snake-shaped petroglyph. The "Serpe de Troña" is on a sacrificial stone with a central channel. One theory is that the blood of the sacrificed victim was spilled into the engraving of the serpent. Other theories state that it is nothing more than a symbol that offered peace and tranquility for the settlement. 

Further Reading: https://www.galiciamaxica.eu/galicia/pontevedra/comarca-do-condado/ponteareas/trona/
